reviews.review_msg: The course really dives into the math behind ML which could be very rough, but he taught it very well. Slides were very good, he went through and wrote out all the steps in great detail which really helped with my understanding of the material and the derivations of the many equations. He's a nice guy too. Could do a lot worse than Mo.

This is a Machine Learning course, but it is mostly heavy math. I was expecting more on the coding side, but there was none. The course is not helpful at all when now I am looking for ML job I have 0 coding knowledge! The final did not make any sense and was not related to its final materials.

His teaching is lackluster. At the start, over 200 students attended; by the end, only about 50 remained. He relies heavily on mental math and moves too fast for students to follow. His favorite phrase is “hopefully it makes sense” before moving on. In the end, he became Santa Claus and was generous with grades.

I hate him!

The course is academically dense but practically hollow. It substitutes deep mathematical derivation for actual conceptual understanding, resulting in a curriculum that is disconnected from modern ML engineering.
Extremely difficult final; avoid at all costs!

Mo is highly intelligent and knowledgeable. He sometimes makes one mistake and that is assuming everyone has the same level of intelligence as him. Therefore, the level of his courses might be to tough for some people at SFU. But he is an amazing and kind person. It was a pleasure for me to get to know and work and take courses with him.
Awful professor. He made the final exam so challenging and didn't bother to scale up the course...
The lectures are not helpful. He reads off from the slides, without explaining why or how. Avoid this class.
Mo's homework often requires readings and material not explicitly taught in class. This is further exacerbated by the lack of a requirement of STAT 270 and MATH 251.
Avoid him, he can't teach machine learning at all.. the lectures are waste of time
The course itself may not be that interesting (lots of math) but Professor Chen explains everything well and clearly. Also, he is patient during the office hour.

He explained mathematic in a very clear way and this course is really useful if you'd like to take the other 700-level courses, e.g. NLP, CG. 
However, this course is heavy of maths and includes lots of quizzes. If you are not confident in math skill, I would suggest to audit instead of enrolment.
Good luck for those also interested in ML. :)

He shows pre-recorded lectures in which he dispassionately reads math off slides.
Does not explain tough concepts properly.
Will never take it again.
